      20              :  * COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER

      24              :  * https://www.FreeRTOS.org
      25              :  * https://github.com/FreeRTOS
      26              :  *
      27              :  */
      28              : 
      29              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------
      30              : * Implementation of functions defined in portable.h for the Posix port.
      31              : *
      32              : * Each task has a pthread which eases use of standard debuggers
      33              : * (allowing backtraces of tasks etc). Threads for tasks that are not
      34              : * running are blocked in sigwait().
      35              : *
      36              : * Task switch is done by resuming the thread for the next task by
      37              : * signaling the condition variable and then waiting on a condition variable
      38              : * with the current thread.
      39              : *
      40              : * The timer interrupt uses SIGALRM and care is taken to ensure that
      41              : * the signal handler runs only on the thread for the current task.
      42              : *
      43              : * Use of part of the standard C library requires care as some
      44              : * functions can take pthread mutexes internally which can result in
      45              : * deadlocks as the FreeRTOS kernel can switch tasks while they're
      46              : * holding a pthread mutex.
      47              : *
      48              : * stdio (printf() and friends) should be called from a single task
      49              : * only or serialized with a FreeRTOS primitive such as a binary
      50              : * semaphore or mutex.
      51              : *----------------------------------------------------------*/
      52              : #include "portmacro.h"
      53              : 
      54              : #ifdef __linux__
      55              :     #define __USE_GNU
      56              : #endif
      57              : 
      58              : #include <errno.h>
      59              : #include <pthread.h>
      60              : #include <limits.h>
      61              : #include <signal.h>
      62              : #include <stdio.h>
      63              : #include <stdlib.h>
      64              : #include <string.h>
      65              : #include <sys/time.h>
      66              : #include <sys/times.h>
      67              : #include <time.h>
      68              : #include <unistd.h>
      69              : 
      70              : #ifdef __APPLE__
      71              :     #include <mach/mach_vm.h>
      72              : #endif
      73              : 
      74              : /* Scheduler includes. */
      75              : #include "FreeRTOS.h"
      76              : #include "task.h"
      77              : #include "timers.h"
      78              : #include "utils/wait_for_event.h"
      79              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
      80              : 
      81              : #define SIG_RESUME    SIGUSR1
      82              : 
      83              : typedef struct THREAD
      84              : {
      85              :     pthread_t pthread;
      86              :     TaskFunction_t pxCode;
      87              :     void * pvParams;
      88              :     BaseType_t xDying;
      89              :     struct event * ev;
      90              : } Thread_t;
      91              : 
      92              : /*
      93              :  * The additional per-thread data is stored at the beginning of the
      94              :  * task's stack.
      95              :  */
      96        32236 : static inline Thread_t * prvGetThreadFromTask( TaskHandle_t xTask )
      97              : {
      98        32236 :     StackType_t * pxTopOfStack = *( StackType_t ** ) xTask;
      99              : 
     100        32236 :     return ( Thread_t * ) ( pxTopOfStack + 1 );
     101              : }
     102              : 
     103              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     104              : 
     105              : static pthread_once_t hSigSetupThread = PTHREAD_ONCE_INIT;
     106              : static sigset_t xAllSignals;
     107              : static sigset_t xSchedulerOriginalSignalMask;
     108              : static pthread_t hMainThread = ( pthread_t ) NULL;
     109              : static volatile BaseType_t uxCriticalNesting;
     110              : static BaseType_t xSchedulerEnd = pdFALSE;
     111              : static pthread_t hTimerTickThread;
     112              : static bool xTimerTickThreadShouldRun;
     113              : static uint64_t prvStartTimeNs;

     117              : static void prvSetupTimerInterrupt( void );

     120              :                              Thread_t * xThreadToSuspend );

     129              :                            int iErrno ) __attribute__( ( __noreturn__ ) );

     135            0 :     abort();
     136              : }

     138              : 
     139           14 : static void prvPortSetCurrentThreadName(char * pxThreadName)
     140              : {
     141              : #ifdef __APPLE__
     142              :     pthread_setname_np(pxThreadName);
     143              : #else
     144           14 :     pthread_setname_np(pthread_self(), pxThreadName);

     146           14 : }

     148              : 
     149              : /*
     150              :  * See header file for description.
     151              :  */
     152           12 : StackType_t * pxPortInitialiseStack( StackType_t * pxTopOfStack,
     153              :                                      StackType_t * pxEndOfStack,
     154              :                                      TaskFunction_t pxCode,
     155              :                                      void * pvParameters )
     156              : {
     157              :     Thread_t * thread;
     158              :     pthread_attr_t xThreadAttributes;
     159              :     size_t ulStackSize;
     160              :     int iRet;
     161              : 
     162           12 :     ( void ) pthread_once( &hSigSetupThread, prvSetupSignalsAndSchedulerPolicy );
     163              : 
     164              :     /*
     165              :      * Store the additional thread data at the start of the stack.
     166              :      */
     167           12 :     thread = ( Thread_t * ) ( pxTopOfStack + 1 ) - 1;
     168           12 :     pxTopOfStack = ( StackType_t * ) thread - 1;
     169              : 
     170              :     #ifdef __APPLE__
     171              :         pxEndOfStack = ( StackType_t * ) mach_vm_round_page( pxEndOfStack );
     172              :     #endif
     173              : 
     174           12 :     ulStackSize = ( size_t ) ( pxTopOfStack + 1 - pxEndOfStack ) * sizeof( *pxTopOfStack );
     175              : 
     176              :     #ifdef __APPLE__
     177              :         ulStackSize = mach_vm_trunc_page( ulStackSize );
     178              :     #endif
     179              : 
     180           12 :     thread->pxCode = pxCode;
     181           12 :     thread->pvParams = pvParameters;
     182           12 :     thread->xDying = pdFALSE;
     183              : 
     184              :     /* Ensure ulStackSize is at least PTHREAD_STACK_MIN */
     185           12 :     ulStackSize = (ulStackSize < PTHREAD_STACK_MIN) ? PTHREAD_STACK_MIN : ulStackSize;
     186              : 
     187           12 :     pthread_attr_init( &xThreadAttributes );
     188           12 :     iRet = pthread_attr_setstacksize( &xThreadAttributes, ulStackSize );
     189              : 
     190           12 :     if( iRet != 0 )
     191              :     {
     192            0 :         fprintf( stderr, "[WARN] pthread_attr_setstacksize failed with return value: %d. Default stack size will be used.\n", iRet );
     193              :     }
     194              : 
     195           12 :     thread->ev = event_create();
     196              : 
     197           12 :     vPortEnterCritical();
     198              : 
     199           12 :     iRet = pthread_create( &thread->pthread, &xThreadAttributes,
     200              :                            prvWaitForStart, thread );
     201              : 
     202           12 :     if( iRet != 0 )
     203              :     {
     204            0 :         prvFatalError( "pthread_create", iRet );
     205              :     }
     206              : 
     207           12 :     vPortExitCritical();
     208              : 
     209           12 :     return pxTopOfStack;
     210              : }
     211              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     212              : 
     213            1 : void vPortStartFirstTask( void )
     214              : {
     215            1 :     Thread_t * pxFirstThread = prvGetThreadFromTask( xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle() );
     216              : 
     217              :     /* Start the first task. */
     218            1 :     prvResumeThread( pxFirstThread );
     219            1 : }
     220              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     221              : 
     222              : /*
     223              :  * See header file for description.
     224              :  */
     225            1 : BaseType_t xPortStartScheduler( void )
     226              : {
     227              :     int iSignal;
     228              :     sigset_t xSignals;
     229              : 
     230            1 :     hMainThread = pthread_self();
     231            1 :     prvPortSetCurrentThreadName("Scheduler");
     232              : 
     233              :     /* Start the timer that generates the tick ISR(SIGALRM).
     234              :      * Interrupts are disabled here already. */
     235            1 :     prvSetupTimerInterrupt();
     236              : 
     237              :     /*
     238              :      * Block SIG_RESUME before starting any tasks so the main thread can sigwait on it.
     239              :      * To sigwait on an unblocked signal is undefined.
     240              :      * https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/009604499/functions/sigwait.html
     241              :      */
     242            1 :     sigemptyset( &xSignals );
     243            1 :     sigaddset( &xSignals, SIG_RESUME );
     244            1 :     ( void ) pthread_sigmask( SIG_BLOCK, &xSignals, NULL );
     245              : 
     246              :     /* Start the first task. */
     247            1 :     vPortStartFirstTask();
     248              : 
     249              :     /* Wait until signaled by vPortEndScheduler(). */
     250            1 :     while( xSchedulerEnd != pdTRUE )
     251              :     {
     252            1 :         sigwait( &xSignals, &iSignal );
     253              :     }
     254              : 
     255              :     /*
     256              :      * clear out the variable that is used to end the scheduler, otherwise
     257              :      * subsequent scheduler restarts will end immediately.
     258              :      */
     259            0 :     xSchedulerEnd = pdFALSE;
     260              : 
     261              :     /* Reset pthread_once_t, needed to restart the scheduler again.
     262              :      * memset the internal struct members for MacOS/Linux Compatability */
     263              :     #if __APPLE__
     264              :         hSigSetupThread.__sig = _PTHREAD_ONCE_SIG_init;
     265              :         memset( ( void * ) &hSigSetupThread.__opaque, 0, sizeof(hSigSetupThread.__opaque));
     266              :     #else /* Linux PTHREAD library*/
     267            0 :         hSigSetupThread = PTHREAD_ONCE_INIT;
     268              :     #endif /* __APPLE__*/
     269              :     
     270              :     /* Restore original signal mask. */
     271            0 :     ( void ) pthread_sigmask( SIG_SETMASK, &xSchedulerOriginalSignalMask, NULL );
     272              : 
     273            0 :     return 0;
     274              : }
     275              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     276              : 
     277            0 : void vPortEndScheduler( void )
     278              : {
     279              :     Thread_t * pxCurrentThread;
     280              : 
     281              :     /* Stop the timer tick thread. */
     282            0 :     xTimerTickThreadShouldRun = false;
     283            0 :     pthread_join( hTimerTickThread, NULL );
     284              : 
     285              :     /* Signal the scheduler to exit its loop. */
     286            0 :     xSchedulerEnd = pdTRUE;
     287            0 :     ( void ) pthread_kill( hMainThread, SIG_RESUME );
     288              : 
     289              :     /* Waiting to be deleted here. */
     290            0 :     pxCurrentThread = prvGetThreadFromTask( xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle() );
     291            0 :     event_wait( pxCurrentThread->ev );
     292            0 :     pthread_testcancel();
     293            0 : }
     294              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     295              : 
     296        16040 : void vPortEnterCritical( void )
     297              : {
     298        16040 :     if( uxCriticalNesting == 0 )
     299              :     {
     300        15812 :         vPortDisableInterrupts();
     301              :     }
     302              : 
     303        16040 :     uxCriticalNesting++;
     304        16040 : }
     305              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     306              : 
     307        16029 : void vPortExitCritical( void )
     308              : {
     309        16029 :     uxCriticalNesting--;
     310              : 
     311              :     /* If we have reached 0 then re-enable the interrupts. */
     312        16029 :     if( uxCriticalNesting == 0 )
     313              :     {
     314        15802 :         vPortEnableInterrupts();
     315              :     }
     316        16029 : }
     317              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     318              : 
     319         1116 : static void prvPortYieldFromISR( void )
     320              : {
     321              :     Thread_t * xThreadToSuspend;
     322              :     Thread_t * xThreadToResume;
     323              : 
     324         1116 :     xThreadToSuspend = prvGetThreadFromTask( xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle() );
     325              : 
     326         1116 :     vTaskSwitchContext();
     327              : 
     328         1116 :     xThreadToResume = prvGetThreadFromTask( xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle() );
     329              : 
     330         1116 :     prvSwitchThread( xThreadToResume, xThreadToSuspend );
     331         1106 : }
     332              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     333              : 
     334         1116 : void vPortYield( void )
     335              : {
     336         1116 :     vPortEnterCritical();
     337              : 
     338         1116 :     prvPortYieldFromISR();
     339              : 
     340         1106 :     vPortExitCritical();
     341         1106 : }
     342              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     343              : 
     344        15813 : void vPortDisableInterrupts( void )
     345              : {
     346        15813 :     pthread_sigmask( SIG_BLOCK, &xAllSignals, NULL );
     347        15813 : }
     348              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     349              : 
     350        15814 : void vPortEnableInterrupts( void )
     351              : {
     352        15814 :     pthread_sigmask( SIG_UNBLOCK, &xAllSignals, NULL );
     353        15814 : }
     354              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     355              : 
     356            0 : UBaseType_t xPortSetInterruptMask( void )
     357              : {
     358              :     /* Interrupts are always disabled inside ISRs (signals
     359              :      * handlers). */
     360            0 :     return ( UBaseType_t ) 0;
     361              : }
     362              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     363              : 
     364            0 : void vPortClearInterruptMask( UBaseType_t uxMask )
     365              : {

     369              : 
     370            1 : static uint64_t prvGetTimeNs( void )
     371              : {
     372              :     struct timespec t;
     373              : 
     374            1 :     clock_gettime( CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t );
     375              : 
     376            1 :     return ( uint64_t ) t.tv_sec * ( uint64_t ) 1000000000UL + ( uint64_t ) t.tv_nsec;
     377              : }
     378              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     379              : 
     380              : /* commented as part of the code below in vPortSystemTickHandler,
     381              :  * to adjust timing according to full demo requirements */
     382              : /* static uint64_t prvTickCount; */
     383              : 
     384            1 : static void * prvTimerTickHandler( void * arg )
     385              : {
     386              :     ( void ) arg;
     387              :     
     388            1 :     prvPortSetCurrentThreadName("Scheduler timer");
     389              : 
     390        10001 :     while( xTimerTickThreadShouldRun )
     391              :     {
     392              :         /*
     393              :          * signal to the active task to cause tick handling or
     394              :          * preemption (if enabled)
     395              :          */
     396        10001 :         Thread_t * thread = prvGetThreadFromTask( xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle() );
     397        10001 :         pthread_kill( thread->pthread, SIGALRM );
     398        10001 :         usleep( portTICK_RATE_MICROSECONDS );
     399              :     }
     400              : 
     401            0 :     return NULL;
     402              : }
     403              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     404              : 
     405              : /*
     406              :  * Setup the systick timer to generate the tick interrupts at the required
     407              :  * frequency.
     408              :  */
     409            1 : void prvSetupTimerInterrupt( void )
     410              : {
     411            1 :     xTimerTickThreadShouldRun = true;
     412            1 :     pthread_create( &hTimerTickThread, NULL, prvTimerTickHandler, NULL );
     413              : 
     414            1 :     prvStartTimeNs = prvGetTimeNs();
     415            1 : }
     416              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     417              : 
     418        10001 : static void vPortSystemTickHandler( int sig )
     419              : {
     420              :     Thread_t * pxThreadToSuspend;
     421              :     Thread_t * pxThreadToResume;
     422              : 
     423              :     ( void ) sig;
     424              : 
     425              : /* uint64_t xExpectedTicks; */
     426              : 
     427        10001 :     uxCriticalNesting++; /* Signals are blocked in this signal handler. */
     428              : 
     429              :     #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 )
     430        10001 :         pxThreadToSuspend = prvGetThreadFromTask( xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle() );
     431              :     #endif
     432              : 
     433              :     /* Tick Increment, accounting for any lost signals or drift in
     434              :      * the timer. */
     435              : 
     436              : /*
     437              :  *      Comment code to adjust timing according to full demo requirements
     438              :  *      xExpectedTicks = (prvGetTimeNs() - prvStartTimeNs)
     439              :  *        / (portTICK_RATE_MICROSECONDS * 1000);
     440              :  * do { */
     441        10001 :     xTaskIncrementTick();
     442              : 
     443              : /*        prvTickCount++;
     444              :  *    } while (prvTickCount < xExpectedTicks);
     445              :  */
     446              : 
     447              :     #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 )
     448              :         /* Select Next Task. */
     449        10001 :         vTaskSwitchContext();
     450              : 
     451        10001 :         pxThreadToResume = prvGetThreadFromTask( xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle() );
     452              : 
     453        10001 :         prvSwitchThread( pxThreadToResume, pxThreadToSuspend );
     454              :     #endif
     455              : 
     456        10000 :     uxCriticalNesting--;
     457        10000 : }
     458              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     459              : 
     460            0 : void vPortThreadDying( void * pxTaskToDelete,
     461              :                        volatile BaseType_t * pxPendYield )
     462              : {
     463            0 :     Thread_t * pxThread = prvGetThreadFromTask( pxTaskToDelete );
     464              : 
     465              :     ( void ) pxPendYield;
     466              : 
     467            0 :     pxThread->xDying = pdTRUE;
     468            0 : }
     469              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     470              : 
     471            0 : void vPortCancelThread( void * pxTaskToDelete )
     472              : {
     473            0 :     Thread_t * pxThreadToCancel = prvGetThreadFromTask( pxTaskToDelete );
     474              : 
     475              :     /*
     476              :      * The thread has already been suspended so it can be safely cancelled.
     477              :      */
     478            0 :     pthread_cancel( pxThreadToCancel->pthread );
     479            0 :     event_signal( pxThreadToCancel->ev );
     480            0 :     pthread_join( pxThreadToCancel->pthread, NULL );
     481            0 :     event_delete( pxThreadToCancel->ev );
     482            0 : }
     483              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     484              : 
     485           12 : static void * prvWaitForStart( void * pvParams )
     486              : {
     487           12 :     Thread_t * pxThread = pvParams;
     488              : 
     489           12 :     prvSuspendSelf( pxThread );
     490              : 
     491              :     /* Resumed for the first time, unblocks all signals. */
     492           12 :     uxCriticalNesting = 0;
     493           12 :     vPortEnableInterrupts();
     494              : 
     495              :     /* Set thread name */
     496           12 :     prvPortSetCurrentThreadName(pcTaskGetName(xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle()));
     497              : 
     498              :     /* Call the task's entry point. */
     499           12 :     pxThread->pxCode( pxThread->pvParams );
     500              : 
     501              :     /* A function that implements a task must not exit or attempt to return to
     502              :      * its caller as there is nothing to return to. If a task wants to exit it
     503              :      * should instead call vTaskDelete( NULL ). Artificially force an assert()
     504              :      * to be triggered if configASSERT() is defined, so application writers can
     505              :      * catch the error. */
     506            0 :     configASSERT( pdFALSE );
     507              : 
     508              :     return NULL;
     509              : }
     510              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     511              : 
     512        11117 : static void prvSwitchThread( Thread_t * pxThreadToResume,
     513              :                              Thread_t * pxThreadToSuspend )
     514              : {
     515              :     BaseType_t uxSavedCriticalNesting;
     516              : 
     517        11117 :     if( pxThreadToSuspend != pxThreadToResume )
     518              :     {
     519              :         /*
     520              :          * Switch tasks.
     521              :          *
     522              :          * The critical section nesting is per-task, so save it on the
     523              :          * stack of the current (suspending thread), restoring it when
     524              :          * we switch back to this task.
     525              :          */
     526         1216 :         uxSavedCriticalNesting = uxCriticalNesting;
     527              : 
     528         1216 :         prvResumeThread( pxThreadToResume );
     529              : 
     530         1216 :         if( pxThreadToSuspend->xDying == pdTRUE )
     531              :         {
     532            0 :             pthread_exit( NULL );
     533              :         }
     534              : 
     535         1216 :         prvSuspendSelf( pxThreadToSuspend );
     536              : 
     537         1205 :         uxCriticalNesting = uxSavedCriticalNesting;
     538              :     }
     539        11106 : }
     540              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     541              : 
     542         1228 : static void prvSuspendSelf( Thread_t * thread )
     543              : {
     544              :     /*
     545              :      * Suspend this thread by waiting for a pthread_cond_signal event.
     546              :      *
     547              :      * A suspended thread must not handle signals (interrupts) so
     548              :      * all signals must be blocked by calling this from:
     549              :      *
     550              :      * - Inside a critical section (vPortEnterCritical() /
     551              :      *   vPortExitCritical()).
     552              :      *
     553              :      * - From a signal handler that has all signals masked.
     554              :      *
     555              :      * - A thread with all signals blocked with pthread_sigmask().
     556              :      */
     557         1228 :     event_wait( thread->ev );
     558         1217 :     pthread_testcancel();
     559         1217 : }
     560              : 
     561              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     562              : 
     563         1217 : static void prvResumeThread( Thread_t * xThreadId )
     564              : {
     565         1217 :     if( pthread_self() != xThreadId->pthread )
     566              :     {
     567         1217 :         event_signal( xThreadId->ev );
     568              :     }
     569         1217 : }
     570              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     571              : 
     572            1 : static void prvSetupSignalsAndSchedulerPolicy( void )
     573              : {
     574              :     struct sigaction sigtick;
     575              :     int iRet;
     576              : 
     577            1 :     hMainThread = pthread_self();
     578              : 
     579              :     /* Initialise common signal masks. */
     580            1 :     sigfillset( &xAllSignals );
     581              : 
     582              :     /* Don't block SIGINT so this can be used to break into GDB while
     583              :      * in a critical section. */
     584            1 :     sigdelset( &xAllSignals, SIGINT );
     585              : 
     586              :     /*
     587              :      * Block all signals in this thread so all new threads
     588              :      * inherits this mask.
     589              :      *
     590              :      * When a thread is resumed for the first time, all signals
     591              :      * will be unblocked.
     592              :      */
     593            1 :     ( void ) pthread_sigmask( SIG_SETMASK,
     594              :                               &xAllSignals,
     595              :                               &xSchedulerOriginalSignalMask );
     596              : 
     597            1 :     sigtick.sa_flags = 0;
     598            1 :     sigtick.sa_handler = vPortSystemTickHandler;
     599            1 :     sigfillset( &sigtick.sa_mask );
     600              : 
     601            1 :     iRet = sigaction( SIGALRM, &sigtick, NULL );
     602              : 
     603            1 :     if( iRet == -1 )
     604              :     {
     605            0 :         prvFatalError( "sigaction", errno );
     606              :     }
     607            1 : }
     608              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
     609              : 
     610        11117 : uint32_t ulPortGetRunTime( void )
     611              : {
     612              :     struct tms xTimes;
     613              : 
     614        11117 :     times( &xTimes );
     615              : 
     616        11117 :     return ( uint32_t ) xTimes.tms_utime;
     617              : }
     618              : /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/

Generated by: LCOV version 2.0-1
